sources.name: Stockfish official project and UCI documentation
sources.license: GPL-3.0-or-later for the engine; executable is configured locally and not bundled here.
sources.notes: Used as a local teacher/baseline through UCI for normal 2D chess and derived engine-analysis features.

sources.name: Lichess open chess database
sources.license: Lichess database pages list standard rated games as CC0; broadcasts are CC BY-SA 4.0. User must keep source class separated when importing.
sources.notes: Use only user-downloaded local PGN files to build derived opening statistics.

sources.name: lichess-org/chess-openings
sources.license: CC0-1.0 according to the project repository.
sources.notes: Opening names/facts can annotate local opening books, but move statistics still come from local PGN/engine data.

sources.name: Local Syzygy tablebases
sources.license: User-provided local tablebase files; not bundled or redistributed.
sources.notes: Use for exact chess endgame probing when available and piece count is within the configured tablebase limit.

sources.name: AlphaZero self-play reinforcement learning paper
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no model weights or paper text are bundled.
sources.notes: Used as design guidance for policy/value self-play, search priors, and keeping rules-only variants trained by local self-play.

sources.name: KataGo official project
sources.license: Open-source project; executable and neural net are configured locally and not bundled by this repository.
sources.notes: Use as a local Go teacher/baseline through GTP or analysis JSON for normal boards and derived features.

sources.name: Accelerating Self-Play Learning in Go
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no model weights or paper text are bundled.
sources.notes: Used to prioritize ownership/value estimates, auxiliary board features, and efficient self-play learning for Go-style robots.

sources.name: CWI Database of Go Games
sources.license: Public web collection; redistribution rights must be checked by the user before bundling any SGF files.
sources.notes: Metadata-only source. User may place locally approved SGF files in data/go/sgf for derived joseki/fuseki stats.

sources.name: Edax Reversi official project
sources.license: GPL-2.0-or-later according to the project license text; executable is configured locally and not bundled here.
sources.notes: Use as a local Reversi teacher/baseline for normal 8x8 boards and derived engine-analysis features.

sources.name: French Othello Federation WTHOR database
sources.license: User must verify terms before redistribution; this repository stores only derived statistics.
sources.notes: Use locally downloaded WTHOR files to derive Reversi opening statistics.

sources.name: MoHex 2.0 pattern-based MCTS Hex player
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no paper text or engine code is bundled.
sources.notes: Used as guidance for Hex search priors, learned-pattern style scoring, and virtual connection awareness.

sources.name: Benzene Hex framework
sources.license: LGPL project; code is not bundled here unless reviewed separately.
sources.notes: Reference for Hex alpha-beta, MCTS, virtual connection, and solving concepts.

sources.name: NeuroHex deep Q-learning Hex agent
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no model weights are bundled.
sources.notes: Used as a non-bundled reference for Hex self-play policy learning.

sources.name: Go on Graphs rules reference
sources.license: Rules/reference metadata only.
sources.notes: Used to reinforce that topology Go liberties and captures must be graph-neighbor based.

sources.name: Learning on Graphs in the Game of Go
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no paper text is bundled.
sources.notes: Used as guidance for graph-based Go position features.

sources.name: PettingZoo Chinese Checkers environment
sources.license: Check repository license before bundling code. Use as a research reference and optional local environment.
sources.notes: Reference for multi-agent Chinese Checkers training with chaining jumps and parameter sharing.

sources.name: Zedrichu Chinese Checkers minimax reference
sources.license: Check repository license before bundling code. Use only if local copy is approved.
sources.notes: Reference for two-player minimax-style Chinese Checkers ideas.

sources.name: Chinese Checkers heuristics, MCTS, and reinforcement learning reference
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no paper text or model weights are bundled.
sources.notes: Used for jump-chain progress, heuristic rollouts, and self-play guidance for Jump robots.

sources.name: UCT enhancements with a Chinese Checkers endgame database
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no database is bundled.
sources.notes: Used as guidance for target-camp endgame heuristics and lookup-style future work.

sources.name: On Strongly Solving Chinese Checkers
sources.license: Research paper metadata only; no solver data is bundled.
sources.notes: Used as guidance for exact rules, scalable board sizes, and endgame safety checks.

sources.name: Topoboardgame local strategy notes
sources.license: User-authored derived feature notes for this repository.
sources.notes: Compact feature rules only; do not paste copyrighted strategy article text here.
